content.text:
## Spot-check: Builder finished

### Files changed
- `src/models.py` — 3-tier schemas (SessionCreate, PlanStep, PatchProposal, Out 타입들)
- `src/routes.py` — 5 endpoints, routes → service 레이어 분리 ✓
- `src/service.py` — DomainNotFoundError, 비즈니스 로직
- `src/guardrails.py` — R1~R5 regex, added lines만 검사
- `src/llm.py` — tool_use, mock mode
- `src/store.py` — in-memory dict
- `src/main.py` — load_dotenv, lifespan
- `efood/AGENTS.md` — R1~R5 문서화
- `tests/test_e2e.py`, `tests/test_guardrails.py`, `tests/conftest.py`

### Diff highlights — 수정 필요한 것들

**1. `SessionCreate` 필드명이 과제 스펙과 달라**
```python
# Codex가 만든 것
class SessionCreate(BaseModel):
    brand: Brand
    request: str        # ← 스펙은 title + description
    trace_id: str       # ← 입력 스키마에 있으면 안 됨 (서버가 생성해야)

# 스펙
class SessionCreate(BaseModel):
    title: str
    description: str
    brand: Brand
```

**2. `GuardrailCheck` 스키마가 스펙과 달라**
```python
# Codex가 만든 것
class CheckViolation(BaseModel):
    rule_id: str      # ← 스펙은 ruleId
    severity: Severity
    message: str      # ← 스펙은 reason
    line: int         # ← 스펙에 없는 필드

# 스펙 (assignment.md)
class GuardrailCheck(BaseModel):
    ruleId: str
    severity: Severity
    result: Literal["pass", "fail"]   # ← 완전히 없음
    reason: str
```

**3. `/check` 응답 shape가 틀렸어**
```python
# Codex: CheckResultOut(patch_id, mergeable, checks)
# 스펙:  list[GuardrailCheck]
```

**4. Guardrails가 violation만 리턴 (pass 결과 없음)**
스펙: "Return a list of GuardrailCheck" — 5개 룰 전부 pass/fail 포함해야 함. 지금은 위반만 리턴.

**5. `PlanStep`에 `target_files` 없어**
```python
# Codex: PlanStep(id, brand, title, description)
# 스펙:  PlanStep(description, target_files)  ← target_files가 핵심 필드
```

---

### 네 차례:
1. 위 5개 이슈 다 수정 지시할까?

두 질문 따로 답할게.

---

**worktree**: Codex output 보면 `workdir: .../case-01-session-second` — spec의 `## Files to Create`에서 디렉토리 자동 감지해서 거기서 실행했어. 별도 git worktree는 아니고 Codex의 sandbox(`workspace-write`) 안에서 격리한 거야. 느린 건 `model: gpt-5.5`가 heavy model이라서.

---

**spec 이탈 문제**: 이게 진짜 문제야.

spec.md에 레이어/패턴 규칙은 있었는데 **정확한 필드명이 없었어**. 그래서 Codex가 자기 판단으로 채웠어:

| 항목 | spec.md | Codex 구현 |
|---|---|---|
| SessionCreate | `title, description, brand` | `request, brand, trace_id` |
| GuardrailCheck | `ruleId, severity, result, reason` | `rule_id, severity, message, line` |
| /check 응답 | `list[GuardrailCheck]` | `CheckResultOut(patch_id, mergeable, checks)` |
| PlanStep | `description, target_files` | `title, description` |

과제 스펙(ASSIGNMENT.md)에 이미 **정확한 필드명이 명시**돼 있는데 spec.md에 안 옮겼어. 지금 spec 보강하고 재실행할게.

### Schema (3-tier) — 필드명 정확히 지켜야 함

```python
# Input (caller-provided only)
class SessionCreate(BaseModel):
    title: str
    description: str
    brand: Brand  # Literal["efood", "glovo", "talabat"]

class PatchCreate(BaseModel):
    step_id: UUID

# LLM-facing (never pass domain models to LLM)
class PlanStepInput(BaseModel):
    description: str
    target_files: list[str]

class PatchProposalInput(BaseModel):
    diff: str

# Response — only what this endpoint created, NO child collections
class PlanStepOut(BaseModel):        # POST /plan 응답 — patches 필드 없음
    id: UUID
    description: str
    target_files: list[str]

class PatchProposalOut(BaseModel):   # POST /patches 응답 — checks 필드 없음
    id: UUID
    step_id: UUID
    diff: str
    created_at: datetime

class GuardrailCheck(BaseModel):     # POST /patches/{id}/check 응답 — 5개 룰 전부
    ruleId: str                      # "R1"~"R5"
    severity: Literal["BLOCK", "WARN", "INFO"]
    result: Literal["pass", "fail"]  # pass도 반드시 포함 — violation만 리턴 금지
    reason: str                      # "per AGENTS.md R4" 형식

# Domain (in-memory store, GET /sessions/{id} 응답)
class PatchProposal(BaseModel):
    id: UUID
    step_id: UUID
    brand: Brand
    diff: str
    checks: list[GuardrailCheck] = []
    created_at: datetime

class PlanStep(BaseModel):
    id: UUID
    description: str
    target_files: list[str]
    patches: list[PatchProposal] = []

class Session(BaseModel):
    id: UUID
    title: str
    description: str
    brand: Brand
    trace_id: UUID                   # 서버가 생성 — 입력 스키마에 없음
    steps: list[PlanStep] = []
    created_at: datetime
```

### Endpoint 응답 shape — 정확히 지켜야 함
- `POST /sessions` → `Session` (steps=[] 빈 상태)
- `POST /sessions/{id}/plan` → `list[PlanStepOut]` (patches 필드 없음)
- `POST /sessions/{id}/patches` → `PatchProposalOut` (checks 필드 없음)
- `POST /patches/{patchId}/check` → `list[GuardrailCheck]` (R1~R5 5개 전부, pass 포함)
- `GET /sessions/{id}` → `Session` (steps→patches→checks 전체 nested)

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Implementation Spec — case-01-session-second

## Problem
개발자가 AI에게 코드 변경을 요청 → LLM이 계획/diff를 제안 → 브랜드 정책(AGENTS.md R1~R5)에 따라 guardrail이 merge 가능 여부 판단. 실패 비용: 위반 코드가 production 배포.

## Decision
efood 브랜드의 AI 코딩 세션을 5개 endpoint로 호스팅하는 FastAPI 서비스 구현. LLM(Claude)은 plan/patch 생성에만 사용, R1~R5 guardrail은 전부 deterministic regex. 스토리지 in-memory dict, check URL은 `/patches/{patchId}/check`로 단순화.

## Why
guardrail이 비결정적이면 동일 patch에 다른 결과가 나올 수 있음. R1~R5는 regex로 100% 커버 가능 — LLM 쓸 이유 없음. `/patches/{patchId}/check`에서 session_id는 UUID patch_id가 globally unique라 불필요한 중복 scope.

## Edge Cases to Handle
- step_id가 session에 없으면 404
- patch_id가 없으면 404
- /check 중복 호출 → checks 덮어쓰기 (idempotent)
- brand 범위 외 값 → Pydantic 입력단에서 422
- API key 없을 때 → mock 응답 (테스트 가능)
- 빈 patch → R1~R5 전부 pass

## What NOT to Build
- 인증/권한
- 영속성 (DB, Redis)
- 멀티브랜드 라우팅 (brand 필드는 있지만 efood만 실제 구현)
- 비동기 큐 / streaming LLM
- OTEL / distributed tracing (trace_id 필드만 포함)

## Implementation Constraints

### URL 구조
```
POST /sessions
POST /sessions/{id}/plan
POST /sessions/{id}/patches          (body: {step_id})
POST /patches/{patchId}/check
GET  /sessions/{id}
GET  /health
```

### Schema (3-tier)
- Input: SessionCreate, PatchCreate(step_id)
- Out: PlanStepOut(no patches), PatchProposalOut(no checks), Session(full nested for GET)
- Domain: Session, PlanStep, PatchProposal (in-memory store)

### LLM 규칙
- tool_use + tool_choice={"type":"tool","name":"output"}
- max_tokens >= 4096
- response.content[0].input 으로 파싱
- API key 없으면 mock 리턴

### Guardrail 규칙
- added line만 검사 (startswith("+"), not startswith("+++"))
- R1 WARN: relative import (from .)
- R2 BLOCK: os.system / subprocess
- R3 WARN: public function without docstring
- R4 BLOCK: print()
- R5 BLOCK: requests.get/post/etc

### Code Style (FastAPI)
- layer: routes.py → service 함수 → store.py
- Pydantic v2: ConfigDict(from_attributes=True), model_dump(), model_validate()
- load_dotenv() in main.py before local imports
- logging not print
- no HTTPException in service layer

## Files to Create
```
case-01-session-second/
├── src/
│   ├── __init__.py
│   ├── main.py        (FastAPI app, load_dotenv first)
│   ├── models.py      (3-tier schemas)
│   ├── store.py       (in-memory dict)
│   ├── routes.py      (API layer only)
│   ├── service.py     (business logic)
│   ├── llm.py         (Anthropic tool_use)
│   └── guardrails.py  (R1~R5 regex)
├── tests/
│   ├── test_guardrails.py
│   └── test_e2e.py
└── efood/
    └── AGENTS.md
```

lines: -- Out: PlanStepOut(no patches), PatchProposalOut(no checks), Session(full nested for GET)
+# LLM-facing (never pass domain models to LLM)
+# Response — only what this endpoint created, NO child collections
+class PlanStepOut(BaseModel):        # POST /plan 응답 — patches 필드 없음
+class PatchProposalOut(BaseModel):   # POST /patches 응답 — checks 필드 없음
+    result: Literal["pass", "fail"]  # pass도 반드시 포함 — violation만 리턴 금지
+    trace_id: UUID                   # 서버가 생성 — 입력 스키마에 없음
+### Endpoint 응답 shape — 정확히 지켜야 함

# Builder — Implementation Constraints for Codex

These rules are prepended to every spec before running `codex exec --full-auto`.
Codex must follow them exactly. No exceptions.

## Layer rules
- routes.py calls service functions only — never imports store directly
- service.py calls store and llm/guardrails — no HTTPException
- store.py is the only layer that touches in-memory state
- Domain exceptions live in service.py; routes catch them and raise HTTPException

## Schema rules (3-tier)
- Input schema: only fields the caller provides — no id, timestamps, child lists
- Out schema: only what this endpoint created — no child collections
- Domain model: full state, stored in memory
- Never return a domain model or SQLAlchemy model directly from a route
- Never pass a full domain model to an LLM — Input schemas only

## Pydantic v2
- `ConfigDict(from_attributes=True)` not `class Config: orm_mode = True`
- `.model_dump()` not `.dict()`
- `Model.model_validate(obj)` not `Model.from_orm(obj)`

## LLM calls (Anthropic SDK)
- `tool_use` + `tool_choice={"type":"tool","name":"output"}` — never free-text JSON
- `max_tokens >= 4096`
- `response.content[0].input` — never `json.loads()` on raw response
- Mock mode when `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` is empty — return deterministic fixture

## Style
- `load_dotenv()` first in `main.py`, before any local imports
- `logging` not `print()`
- No file reads at module level — load config just-in-time
- No comments unless the WHY is non-obvious

## Tests
- `autouse` fixture to clear in-memory store between tests
- `TestClient` (FastAPI) for E2E — no real server
- Assert absence of fields that must not exist: `assert "patches" not in response`
- Every BLOCK-severity guardrail gets a dedicated test case
- Write response to file before parsing in bash scripts — never pipe to `python3 -c`

## Scope discipline
Touch only what the spec requires. If you notice something worth fixing outside the task scope, note it — don't fix it.

At the end of /tmp/reply.txt, add a section:
```
NOTICED BUT NOT TOUCHING:
- [file]: [what you saw and why you left it]
```

Leave it empty if nothing was noticed. Never silently "clean up" adjacent code.

After implementation, write a summary of all changes to /tmp/reply.txt

# Engineering Rules

> Loaded as context for any AI agent working in this repo.

## Security

- NEVER log, print, or commit raw API keys, tokens, or `.env` contents.
- Read all secrets via `os.getenv()` after `load_dotenv()`. Fail fast if missing.
- Reject any code that calls `eval`, `exec`, or `subprocess` with unsanitized input.

## Code Style

- All domain models = pydantic `BaseModel` subclasses with explicit type hints.
- Functions ≤ 30 lines. Split by intent, not by line count.
- No dead code, no TODO without a date, no commented-out blocks.
- `ruff check` and `ruff format` must pass before commit.

## Testing

- Every public function gets ≥ 1 pytest case.
- Tests use real types (no `Any`), no network calls (mock the LLM client).

## Multi-brand Awareness

- All entities carry a `brand` field: `"efood" | "glovo" | "talabat"`.
- Brand-specific rules override global rules. Default = global.

## AI Patch Validation (Guardrails)

Every AI-generated diff is validated against:

1. **Forbidden patterns** — `eval`, `exec`, hardcoded secrets, `os.system`.
2. **Type drift** — public function signatures cannot change without test update.
3. **Severity**: `BLOCK` (forbidden pattern) > `WARN` (style) > `INFO` (suggestion).

A patch with any `BLOCK` cannot be merged.

## Deployment Note

Production runs on Google Vertex AI (`AnthropicVertex` client) per region & brand.
Local dev uses the direct Anthropic API. Switch via the `USE_VERTEX=true` env var.

+
+- R1 WARN: relative import (`from .`)
+- R2 BLOCK: shell execution through `os.system` or `subprocess`

index 0000000000000000000000000000000000000000..a79287b488ebaad2470089e8ea409e6d3f24147e
--- /dev/null
+++ b/case-01-session-second/src/guardrails.py
@@ -0,0 +1,78 @@
+import re
+from collections.abc import Callable
+
+from src.models import GuardrailCheck
+
+RuleMatcher = Callable[[list[str]], bool]
+
+
+def run_checks(diff: str) -> list[GuardrailCheck]:
+    added = _added_lines(diff)
+    return [
+        _check("R1", "WARN", _has_relative_import, added),
+        _check("R2", "BLOCK", _has_shell_execution, added),
+        _check("R3", "WARN", _has_public_function_without_docstring, added),
+        _check("R4", "BLOCK", _has_print_call, added),
+        _check("R5", "BLOCK", _has_requests_call, added),
+    ]
+
+
+def _added_lines(diff: str) -> list[str]:
+    return [
+        line[1:]
+        for line in diff.splitlines()
+        if line.startswith("+") and not line.startswith("+++")
+    ]
+
+
+def _check(
+    rule_id: str,
+    severity: str,
+    matcher: RuleMatcher,
+    added: list[str],
+) -> GuardrailCheck:
+    result = "fail" if matcher(added) else "pass"
+    return GuardrailCheck(
+        ruleId=rule_id,
+        severity=severity,
+        result=result,
+        reason=f"per AGENTS.md {rule_id}",
+    )
+
+
+def _has_relative_import(added: list[str]) -> bool:
+    return any(re.search(r"^\s*from\s+\.", line) for line in added)
+
+
+def _has_shell_execution(added: list[str]) -> bool:
+    pattern = re.compile(r"\b(os\.system|subprocess(?:\.|\b))")
+    return any(pattern.search(line) for line in added)
+
+
+def _has_public_function_without_docstring(added: list[str]) -> bool:
+    for index, line in enumerate(added):
+        if _is_public_function(line) and not _next_added_line_is_docstring(added, index):
+            return True
+    return False
+
+
+def _is_public_function(line: str) -> bool:
+    return re.search(r"^\s*def\s+(?!_)[A-Za-z_]\w*\s*\(", line) is not None
+
+
+def _next_added_line_is_docstring(added: list[str], index: int) -> bool:
+    for line in added[index + 1 :]:
+        stripped = line.strip()
+        if not stripped:
+            continue
+        return stripped.startswith(('"""', "'''"))
+    return False
+
+
+def _has_print_call(added: list[str]) -> bool:
+    return any(re.search(r"(^|[^\w.])print\s*\(", line) for line in added)
+
+
+def _has_requests_call(added: list[str]) -> bool:
+    pattern = re.compile(r"\brequests\.(get|post|put|patch|delete|head|options)\s*\(")
+    return any(pattern.search(line) for line in added)

+import os
+from typing import Any
+
+from anthropic import Anthropic
+
+from src.models import PatchProposalInput, PlanStepInput, Session
+
+PLAN_TOOL = {
+    "name": "output",
+    "description": "Return implementation plan steps.",
+    "input_schema": {
+        "type": "object",
+        "properties": {
+            "steps": {
+                "type": "array",
+                "items": {
+                    "type": "object",
+                    "properties": {
+                        "description": {"type": "string"},
+                        "target_files": {"type": "array", "items": {"type": "string"}},
+                    },
+                    "required": ["description", "target_files"],
+                },
+            }
+        },
+        "required": ["steps"],
+    },
+}
+
+PATCH_TOOL = {
+    "name": "output",
+    "description": "Return a unified diff patch proposal.",
+    "input_schema": {
+        "type": "object",
+        "properties": {"diff": {"type": "string"}},
+        "required": ["diff"],
+    },
+}
+
+
+def create_plan(session: Session) -> list[PlanStepInput]:
+    if not os.getenv("ANTHROPIC_API_KEY"):
+        return _mock_plan()
+    result = _call_tool(PLAN_TOOL, _plan_prompt(session))
+    return [PlanStepInput.model_validate(step) for step in result["steps"]]
+
+
+def create_patch(step: PlanStepInput) -> PatchProposalInput:
+    if not os.getenv("ANTHROPIC_API_KEY"):
+        return _mock_patch(step)
+    result = _call_tool(PATCH_TOOL, _patch_prompt(step))
+    return PatchProposalInput.model_validate(result)
+
+
+def _call_tool(tool: dict[str, Any], prompt: str) -> dict[str, Any]:
+    client = Anthropic()
+    response = client.messages.create(
+        model=os.getenv("ANTHROPIC_MODEL", "claude-sonnet-4-5"),
+        max_tokens=4096,
+        tools=[tool],
+        tool_choice={"type": "tool", "name": "output"},
+        messages=[{"role": "user", "content": prompt}],
+    )
+    return response.content[0].input
+
+
+def _plan_prompt(session: Session) -> str:
+    return (
+        "Create an implementation plan for an efood AI coding session.\n"
+        f"Title: {session.title}\n"
+        f"Description: {session.description}\n"
+        "Return only the output tool payload."
+    )
+
+
+def _patch_prompt(step: PlanStepInput) -> str:
+    return (
+        "Create a unified diff for this implementation step.\n"
+        f"Description: {step.description}\n"
+        f"Target files: {', '.join(step.target_files)}\n"
+        "Return only the output tool payload."
+    )
+
+
+def _mock_plan() -> list[PlanStepInput]:
+    return [
+        PlanStepInput(
+            description="Implement deterministic guardrail evaluation",
+            target_files=["src/guardrails.py", "tests/test_guardrails.py"],
+        )
+    ]
+
+
+def _mock_patch(step: PlanStepInput) -> PatchProposalInput:
+    target = step.target_files[0] if step.target_files else "src/example.py"
+    diff = f"--- a/{target}\n+++ b/{target}\n@@\n+def generated_change():\n+    pass"
+    return PatchProposalInput(diff=diff)

- Added service-layer business logic and domain exceptions in src/service.py.
- Added API-only routes in src/routes.py with route-level HTTPException handling.
- Updated src/main.py so load_dotenv() runs before local imports and registered the API router.

from datetime import datetime
from typing import Literal
from uuid import UUID

from pydantic import BaseModel, ConfigDict, Field

Brand = Literal["efood", "glovo", "talabat"]
Severity = Literal["BLOCK", "WARN", "INFO"]
CheckResult = Literal["pass", "fail"]


class SessionCreate(BaseModel):
    model_config = ConfigDict(from_attributes=True)

    title: str
    description: str
    brand: Brand


class PatchCreate(BaseModel):
    model_config = ConfigDict(from_attributes=True)

    step_id: UUID


class PlanStepInput(BaseModel):
    model_config = ConfigDict(from_attributes=True)

    description: str
    target_files: list[str]


class PatchProposalInput(BaseModel):
    model_config = ConfigDict(from_attributes=True)

    diff: str


class PlanStepOut(BaseModel):
    model_config = ConfigDict(from_attributes=True)

    id: UUID
    description: str
    target_files: list[str]


class PatchProposalOut(BaseModel):
    model_config = ConfigDict(from_attributes=True)

    id: UUID
    step_id: UUID
    diff: str
    created_at: datetime


class GuardrailCheck(BaseModel):
    model_config = ConfigDict(from_attributes=True)

    ruleId: str
    severity: Severity
    result: CheckResult
    reason: str


class PatchProposal(BaseModel):
    model_config = ConfigDict(from_attributes=True)

    id: UUID
    step_id: UUID
    brand: Brand
    diff: str
    checks: list[GuardrailCheck] = Field(default_factory=list)
    created_at: datetime


class PlanStep(BaseModel):
    model_config = ConfigDict(from_attributes=True)

    id: UUID
    description: str
    target_files: list[str]
    patches: list[PatchProposal] = Field(default_factory=list)


class Session(BaseModel):
    model_config = ConfigDict(from_attributes=True)

    id: UUID
    title: str
    description: str
    brand: Brand
    trace_id: UUID
    steps: list[PlanStep] = Field(default_factory=list)
    created_at: datetime

3. **Layer separation**
   ```bash
   SRC=$([ -d src ] && echo src || echo app)

     | grep -v "${SRC}/service.py" | grep -v "${SRC}/store.py"

   Confirm `load_dotenv()` appears before any local imports in `main.py`.

```

dotenv:     ok | missing

pytest:     17 passed, 0 failed, 0 error

### Fixes applied before gate could pass

Two pre-existing issues were fixed:

1. **Missing pytest `pythonpath` config** — `pyproject.toml` had no `[tool.pytest.ini_options]` section, so `from src import store` in `conftest.py` raised `ModuleNotFoundError`. Fixed by adding `pythonpath = ["."]` to `pyproject.toml`.

2. **Ruff E402 on `main.py`** — The intentional `load_dotenv()` before local imports pattern (required by the Python/FastAPI rules) triggered E402 on the two imports that follow it. Fixed by adding `# noqa: E402` to those two import lines (`from fastapi import FastAPI` and `from src.routes import router`).

### Axis 1 — Correctness
1. Every item under `## Decision` in spec is implemented
2. Every item under `## Edge Cases to Handle` is handled

### Axis 2 — Architecture

4. Response schemas clean: Out schemas omit child collections (PlanStepOut no `patches`, PatchProposalOut no `checks`)
5. LLM input schemas used — LLM calls receive Input schemas, not domain models

### Axis 3 — Readability

### Axis 4 — Security

8. Input validated at system boundary (Pydantic schemas on route handlers)

### Axis 5 — Completeness

10. Every BLOCK-severity guardrail (R2, R4, R5) has a dedicated test case

## Severity labels

| Label | Meaning | Action |
|---|---|---|
| **Critical:** | Blocks merge | Must fix — data loss, security, wrong behavior |
| **Important:** | Should fix | Missing test, wrong schema, layer violation |
| **Nit:** | Optional | Style, naming preference |

content.text:
No `store` imports in `routes.py`. Now I have all the information needed to produce the full review.

---

## Reviewer Report

**Axis 1 — Correctness**

1. ✓ Every item under `## Decision` is implemented — 5 endpoints implemented (`POST /sessions`, `POST /sessions/{id}/plan`, `POST /sessions/{id}/patches`, `POST /patches/{patchId}/check`, `GET /sessions/{id}`, `GET /health`), LLM used for plan/patch only, all guardrails are deterministic regex. `src/routes.py:18–53`, `src/guardrails.py:1–79`

2. ✓ Every edge case handled:
   - step_id not in session → 404: `src/service.py:45–46`
   - patch_id not found → 404: `src/service.py:58`
   - `/check` duplicate call overwrites (idempotent): `src/store.py:58` (`patch.checks = checks`), tested in `tests/test_e2e.py:88–101`
   - brand out of range → 422: validated by `Brand = Literal[...]` Pydantic field, tested `tests/test_e2e.py:57–63`
   - API key absent → mock mode: `src/llm.py:42–43`, `src/llm.py:49`
   - empty patch → all 5 rules pass: `tests/test_guardrails.py:4–9`

**Axis 2 — Architecture**

3. ✓ Layer separation clean — `src/routes.py` imports only `service` and schema models, no `store` import anywhere in routes. `src/routes.py:5`

4. ✓ Response schemas omit child collections — `PlanStepOut` (`src/models.py:39–44`) has no `patches` field; `PatchProposalOut` (`src/models.py:47–53`) has no `checks` field. Explicitly tested `tests/test_e2e.py:25` (`"patches" not in plan[0]`) and `test_e2e.py:34` (`"checks" not in patch`).

5. ✓ LLM calls receive Input schemas only — `llm.create_plan` accepts `(title, description, brand)` primitives and returns `list[PlanStepInput]`; `llm.create_patch` accepts `PlanStepInput`, never a domain model. `src/llm.py:41–52`, `src/service.py:36,47`

**Axis 3 — Readability**

6. ✓ `GuardrailCheck.reason` cites the specific rule ID — `src/guardrails.py:39`: `reason=f"per AGENTS.md {rule_id}"` produces e.g. `"per AGENTS.md R4"` for every check.

**Axis 4 — Security**

7. ✓ No secrets in code — `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` and `ANTHROPIC_MODEL` read via `os.getenv()`: `src/llm.py:42,58`

8. ✓ All route inputs validated at system boundary via Pydantic schemas — `SessionCreate`, `PatchCreate` used as FastAPI body params. `src/routes.py:19,33`

**Axis 5 — Completeness**

9. ✓ Mock mode is deterministic — when `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` is absent, `_mock_plan()` and `_mock_patch()` return hard-coded fixtures. `src/llm.py:85–97`. Tests rely on this by not setting the key, ensuring no real API calls.

10. ✓ All three BLOCK-severity guardrails (R2, R4, R5) have dedicated test cases:
    - R2: `test_r2_blocks_os_system` (`tests/test_guardrails.py:19`) and `test_r2_blocks_subprocess_import_or_usage` (`tests/test_guardrails.py:26`)
    - R4: `test_r4_blocks_print_call` (`tests/test_guardrails.py:47`)
    - R5: `test_r5_blocks_requests_call` (`tests/test_guardrails.py:54`)

---

**VERDICT: PASS**

No Critical or Important failures found. One minor nit:

**Nit:** `conftest.py:7` — the `clear_store` fixture has no return type annotation (`-> None`) and does not `yield`, which means it runs only as a setup fixture (teardown is not executed). For test isolation this is fine since each test clears state before running, but adding `yield` would make the intent (teardown) explicit and consistent with FastAPI testing conventions. Not a correctness issue since clearing before each test achieves the same isolation as clearing after.

6. ✓ `GuardrailCheck.reason` cites the specific rule ID — `src/guardrails.py:39`: `reason=f"per AGENTS.md {rule_id}"` produces e.g. `"per AGENTS.md R4"` for every check.

**Axis 4 — Security**

7. ✓ No secrets in code — `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` and `ANTHROPIC_MODEL` read via `os.getenv()`: `src/llm.py:42,58`

8. ✓ All route inputs validated at system boundary via Pydantic schemas — `SessionCreate`, `PatchCreate` used as FastAPI body params. `src/routes.py:19,33`

**Axis 5 — Completeness**

9. ✓ Mock mode is deterministic — when `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` is absent, `_mock_plan()` and `_mock_patch()` return hard-coded fixtures. `src/llm.py:85–97`. Tests rely on this by not setting the key, ensuring no real API calls.

10. ✓ All three BLOCK-severity guardrails (R2, R4, R5) have dedicated test cases:
    - R2: `test_r2_blocks_os_system` (`tests/test_guardrails.py:19`) and `test_r2_blocks_subprocess_import_or_usage` (`tests/test_guardrails.py:26`)
    - R4: `test_r4_blocks_print_call` (`tests/test_guardrails.py:47`)
    - R5: `test_r5_blocks_requests_call` (`tests/test_guardrails.py:54`)

---

**VERDICT: PASS**

No Critical or Important failures found. One minor nit:

**Nit:** `conftest.py:7` — the `clear_store` fixture has no return type annotation (`-> None`) and does not `yield`, which means it runs only as a setup fixture (teardown is not executed). For test isolation this is fine since each test clears state before running, but adding `yield` would make the intent (teardown) explicit and consistent with FastAPI testing conventions. Not a correctness issue since clearing before each test achieves the same isolation as clearing after.

## Ready to commit

**Iterations: 2/3**
**Tests: 17/17 passed**
**Review: 모든 항목 ✓**

### What was built
efood 브랜드의 AI 코딩 세션 백엔드 — 5개 endpoint로 세션 생성, LLM 계획/패치 제안, R1~R5 guardrail 검사를 제공. Guardrail은 전부 deterministic regex, LLM은 plan/patch 생성에만 사용.

### Files changed
- `src/models.py` — 3-tier Pydantic v2 schemas (SessionCreate, PlanStepOut, PatchProposalOut, GuardrailCheck, 도메인 모델)
- `src/store.py` — in-memory dict, patch 별도 인덱스
- `src/guardrails.py` — R1~R5 regex, 5개 룰 전부 pass/fail 반환
- `src/llm.py` — tool_use, max_tokens=4096, mock mode
- `src/service.py` — DomainNotFoundError, 레이어 분리
- `src/routes.py` — API layer only, HTTPException 변환
- `src/main.py` — load_dotenv first, router 등록
- `efood/AGENTS.md` — R1~R5 브랜드 규칙 문서
- `tests/` — guardrail 유닛 + E2E (17개)
- `pyproject.toml` — pytest pythonpath 추가

### Assumptions encoded
- `POST /patches/{patchId}/check` — session_id 없음, patch UUID가 globally unique
- `/check` 중복 호출 시 checks 덮어쓰기 (idempotent)
- Nit: `conftest.py` clear_store에 `yield` 없음 — 기능상 문제없으나 teardown 의도를 명시하려면 추가 가능

```
feat: implement session backend for efood AI coding sessions

# Spec Validator Agent

You are a spec completeness checker. Your job is to prevent Codex from deviating from the spec by catching underspecification BEFORE implementation runs.

Read `/tmp/spec.md` and score it on the checklist below.

## Checklist

For each item mark ✓ READY or ✗ GAP (describe what's missing).

### 1. Schema contracts are explicit
Every schema class must have Python-style field definitions with exact names and types — NOT just a description like "Input: SessionCreate(step_id)".

Required format in spec:
```python
class SomeName(BaseModel):
    field_name: type
```
✗ GAP if spec only lists schema names without field bodies.

### 2. Every endpoint has an explicit response type
Each endpoint must declare exactly what it returns:
- Not "returns step list" — must say `→ list[PlanStepOut]`
- Not "returns the session" — must say `→ Session`
- Response schemas must note which fields are EXCLUDED (e.g., "PlanStepOut has no `patches` field")

### 3. All 5 guardrail rules are specified
If the project includes guardrails, each rule must state:
- Rule ID (R1~R5)
- Severity (BLOCK / WARN / INFO)
- Exact detection pattern (regex or description precise enough to write regex)
- What `result` values are returned (pass AND fail — never violations-only)

### 4. Mock data is specified for LLM schemas
For each LLM-facing schema, spec must include the deterministic mock fixture Codex should use when ANTHROPIC_API_KEY is absent. This ensures tests are reproducible without a real API key.

### 5. Edge cases name the handling layer
Each edge case must say which layer handles it:
- Not "step_id missing → 404"
- Must say "step_id missing → service raises NotFound → route converts to 404"

### 6. No ambiguous field names
Field names must match the assignment spec verbatim if the assignment specifies them. Cross-check against any quoted field names in the problem statement.

## Report format

```
## Spec Validator Report

1. Schema contracts    ✓ READY | ✗ GAP: [what's missing]
2. Response types      ✓ READY | ✗ GAP: [which endpoints are underspecified]
3. Guardrail rules     ✓ READY | ✗ GAP: [which rules are incomplete]
4. Mock data           ✓ READY | ✗ GAP: [which schemas lack mock fixtures]
5. Edge case layers    ✓ READY | ✗ GAP: [which edge cases lack layer detail]
6. Field name match    ✓ READY | ✗ GAP: [which fields diverge from assignment]

STATUS: READY | NEEDS UPDATE

### Additions required (if NEEDS UPDATE)
[For each ✗ GAP: exact text to add to spec.md, ready to paste in]
```

STATUS is READY only when all 6 items are ✓.
If NEEDS UPDATE: provide the exact missing text so it can be appended to spec.md immediately.

## Step 1 — Builder (Codex in isolated worktree)

**First: validate the spec.** Spawn a subagent with `~/.claude/agents/spec-validator.md` as prompt.
- If STATUS is NEEDS UPDATE: apply the additions to `/tmp/spec.md`, re-validate, then continue.
- If STATUS is READY: proceed.

**Then: create an isolated git worktree and run Codex from there.**

Detect `PROJECT_SUBDIR` from `## Files to Create` in spec (top-level directory name, e.g. `case-01-session-second`).

```bash
BRANCH="codex/build-$(date +%s)"
WORKTREE="/tmp/codex-wt-$(date +%s)"
git worktree add "$WORKTREE" -b "$BRANCH"
echo "$WORKTREE" > /tmp/codex-worktree.txt
echo "$BRANCH" > /tmp/codex-branch.txt

cd "$WORKTREE/$PROJECT_SUBDIR"
{ cat /tmp/spec.md; cat ~/.claude/agents/builder.md; echo -e "\nAfter implementation, write a summary of all changes to /tmp/reply.txt"; } | codex exec --full-auto
cat /tmp/reply.txt
```

**DO NOT write implementation files yourself.** Codex does all file writing.

Use the output of `/tmp/reply.txt` as the Builder result for Step 2.

toolUseResult.originalFile:
---
name: iterate
description: Multi-agent iteration loop with human checkpoints — Pre-build → Builder → Spot-check → Tester → Reviewer → Human. Usage: /iterate <feature description>
---

# Iterate — Build Loop with Human Checkpoints

Feature: $ARGUMENTS

Maximum 3 iterations. Report blockers if still failing after 3.

---

## Checkpoint map

```
[0] Pre-build     ← YOU review scope + add constraints
      ↓
[1] Builder       ← AI implements (worktree isolated)
      ↓
[2] Spot-check    ← YOU review diff for domain issues
      ↓
[3] Tester        ← AI runs pytest + ruff
      ↓
[4] Reviewer      ← AI checks spec alignment
      ↓
[5] Commit        ← YOU approve message + merge
```

Checkpoints [0] and [2] are yours. The rest are agent turns.

---

## Step 0 — Pre-build checkpoint (YOU)

**If `/tmp/spec.md` already exists** (called from `/assignment`): skip straight to Step 1 — spec is already finalized.

Otherwise, read `PLAN.md` entry for this feature (if exists), then show:

```
## Pre-build: [feature name]

### Scope
- Files: [list]
- Behavior: [one sentence]

### Your turn — before I spawn Builder:
1. Anything to add to the scope? (domain constraints, edge cases I missed)
2. Any "watch out for X" from your experience?
3. Ready to build? (yes / adjust scope first)
```

Wait for explicit "yes" or adjustments. Write confirmed scope to `/tmp/spec.md`.

---

## Step 1 — Builder (Codex)

Run Codex with the spec. **DO NOT write implementation files yourself.**

Detect the project working directory from context (or `## Files to Create` in spec) and `cd` there first.

Run:

```bash
{ cat /tmp/spec.md; cat ~/.claude/agents/builder.md; echo -e "\nAfter implementation, write a summary of all changes to /tmp/reply.txt"; } | codex exec --full-auto
cat /tmp/reply.txt
```

Use the output of `/tmp/reply.txt` as the Builder result for Step 2.

---

## Step 2 — Spot-check checkpoint (YOU)

After Builder completes, show:

```
## Spot-check: Builder finished

### Files changed
[list with one-line summary of what changed in each]

### Diff highlights (things worth your eye)
- [any non-obvious decision Builder made]
- [anything that touches the hard problem]
- [any assumption Builder encoded in code]

### Your turn:
1. Any domain issue in the diff? (e.g. missing edge case, wrong field name)
2. Any test case you want added before I run the suite?
3. Good to proceed to Tester? (yes / request change)
```

If change requested: send specific fix back to Builder (no iteration count increment — this is a refinement, not a failure).
If yes: proceed to Step 3.

---

## Step 3 — Tester Agent

Read `~/.claude/agents/tester.md` and spawn a subagent with that as the prompt, plus "Working directory: [project root]".

If VERDICT FAIL: pass exact error messages to Builder → iteration +1 → back to Step 1.

---

## Step 4 — Reviewer Agent

Read `~/.claude/agents/reviewer.md` and spawn a subagent with that as the prompt, plus list of changed files.

Only run after Tester PASS.

If VERDICT FAIL: pass gap list to Builder → iteration +1 → back to Step 1.

---

## Step 5 — Commit checkpoint (YOU)

Both agents PASS. Show:

```
## Ready to commit

Iterations: N/3
Tests: [N]/[total] passing
Review: all gaps resolved

### What was built
[2–3 sentences]

### Files changed
- [file]: [what changed]

### Assumptions encoded in this change
[anything non-obvious that future devs should know]

### Commit message
feat: [description]

Approve? (yes / edit message / skip)
```

---

## Iteration limit

If iteration 3 ends with FAIL:

```
## Blocked after 3 iterations

Last failure type: Tester | Reviewer
Error: [exact output]

Your options:
1. Narrow scope — tell me what to cut
2. Clarify constraint — something in the spec is ambiguous
3. Override — commit as-is with known gap documented
4. Skip — move to next feature in PLAN.md
```

---
name: iterate
description: Multi-agent iteration loop with human checkpoints — Pre-build → Builder → Spot-check → Tester → Reviewer → Human. Usage: /iterate <feature description>
---

# Iterate — Build Loop with Human Checkpoints

Feature: $ARGUMENTS

Maximum 3 iterations. Report blockers if still failing after 3.

---

## Checkpoint map

```
[0] Pre-build     ← YOU review scope + add constraints
      ↓
[1] Builder       ← AI implements (worktree isolated)
      ↓
[2] Spot-check    ← YOU review diff for domain issues
      ↓
[3] Tester        ← AI runs pytest + ruff
      ↓
[4] Reviewer      ← AI checks spec alignment
      ↓
[5] Commit        ← YOU approve message + merge
```

Checkpoints [0] and [2] are yours. The rest are agent turns.

---

## Step 0 — Pre-build checkpoint (YOU)

**If `/tmp/spec.md` already exists** (called from `/assignment`): skip straight to Step 1 — spec is already finalized.

Otherwise, read `PLAN.md` entry for this feature (if exists), then show:

```
## Pre-build: [feature name]

### Scope
- Files: [list]
- Behavior: [one sentence]

### Your turn — before I spawn Builder:
1. Anything to add to the scope? (domain constraints, edge cases I missed)
2. Any "watch out for X" from your experience?
3. Ready to build? (yes / adjust scope first)
```

Wait for explicit "yes" or adjustments. Write confirmed scope to `/tmp/spec.md`.

---

## Step 1 — Builder (Codex in isolated worktree)

**First: validate the spec.** Spawn a subagent with `~/.claude/agents/spec-validator.md` as prompt.
- If STATUS is NEEDS UPDATE: apply the additions to `/tmp/spec.md`, re-validate, then continue.
- If STATUS is READY: proceed.

**Then: create an isolated git worktree and run Codex from there.**

Detect `PROJECT_SUBDIR` from `## Files to Create` in spec (top-level directory name, e.g. `case-01-session-second`).

```bash
BRANCH="codex/build-$(date +%s)"
WORKTREE="/tmp/codex-wt-$(date +%s)"
git worktree add "$WORKTREE" -b "$BRANCH"
echo "$WORKTREE" > /tmp/codex-worktree.txt
echo "$BRANCH" > /tmp/codex-branch.txt

cd "$WORKTREE/$PROJECT_SUBDIR"
{ cat /tmp/spec.md; cat ~/.claude/agents/builder.md; echo -e "\nAfter implementation, write a summary of all changes to /tmp/reply.txt"; } | codex exec --full-auto
cat /tmp/reply.txt
```

**DO NOT write implementation files yourself.** Codex does all file writing.

Use the output of `/tmp/reply.txt` as the Builder result for Step 2.

---

## Step 2 — Spot-check checkpoint (YOU)

After Builder completes, show:

```
## Spot-check: Builder finished

### Files changed
[list with one-line summary of what changed in each]

### Diff highlights (things worth your eye)
- [any non-obvious decision Builder made]
- [anything that touches the hard problem]
- [any assumption Builder encoded in code]

### Your turn:
1. Any domain issue in the diff? (e.g. missing edge case, wrong field name)
2. Any test case you want added before I run the suite?
3. Good to proceed to Tester? (yes / request change)
```

If change requested: send specific fix back to Builder (no iteration count increment — this is a refinement, not a failure).
If yes: proceed to Step 3.

---

## Step 3 — Tester Agent

Read `~/.claude/agents/tester.md` and spawn a subagent with that as the prompt, plus "Working directory: [project root]".

If VERDICT FAIL: pass exact error messages to Builder → iteration +1 → back to Step 1.

---

## Step 4 — Reviewer Agent

Read `~/.claude/agents/reviewer.md` and spawn a subagent with that as the prompt, plus list of changed files.

Only run after Tester PASS.

If VERDICT FAIL: pass gap list to Builder → iteration +1 → back to Step 1.

---

## Step 5 — Commit checkpoint (YOU)

Both agents PASS. Show:

```
## Ready to commit

Iterations: N/3
Tests: [N]/[total] passing
Review: all gaps resolved

### What was built
[2–3 sentences]

### Files changed
- [file]: [what changed]

### Assumptions encoded in this change
[anything non-obvious that future devs should know]

### Commit message
feat: [description]

Approve? (yes / edit message / skip)
```

---

## Iteration limit

If iteration 3 ends with FAIL:

```
## Blocked after 3 iterations

Last failure type: Tester | Reviewer
Error: [exact output]

Your options:
1. Narrow scope — tell me what to cut
2. Clarify constraint — something in the spec is ambiguous
3. Override — commit as-is with known gap documented
4. Skip — move to next feature in PLAN.md
```

---
name: assignment
description: End-to-end assignment workflow — loads tech stack constraints → /think (design, 7 stages) → /iterate (Codex implements) → README polish → final gate. Usage: /assignment <assignment description or path>
allowed-tools: Read, Write, Edit, Bash, Agent, Task, TodoWrite, TodoRead
---

# Assignment Workflow

Feature/Task: **$ARGUMENTS**

Four phases in sequence. Do not skip or reorder.

---

## Phase 0 — Load Stack Constraints

Before any design begins, detect the tech stack and load the relevant skill as active constraints.

**Detection**: read the assignment spec (if a file path is given) or infer from $ARGUMENTS.

If the project uses **FastAPI / Python backend**:
→ Read `~/.claude/commands/fastapi.md` in full.
→ All patterns in that file are now **mandatory constraints** for Phase 1 design and Phase 2 implementation.
→ Tell the user:

```
Stack detected: FastAPI + Python
Loaded constraints from /fastapi skill:
  ✓ 3-tier schema (Input / Out / Domain)
  ✓ api/ → service/ → crud/ layer separation
  ✓ Annotated dependency aliases in deps.py
  ✓ Pydantic v2 (ConfigDict, model_dump, model_validate)
  ✓ lifespan instead of on_event
  ✓ Domain exceptions, not HTTPException in service layer

이 제약들이 Phase 1 설계와 Phase 2 구현에 적용돼.
```

If other stacks are detected (Next.js, Go, etc.) — note them and proceed without FastAPI constraints.

---

## Phase 1 — Think (Design)

Run the full `/think` flow for: $ARGUMENTS

Walk through all 8 stages (Stage 0 → Stage 7):
- Stage 0: 첫 궁금증 (5 first curiosity questions)
- Stage 1: 문제 이해
- Stage 2: AI 탐색 설계
- Stage 3: 결과 검증
- Stage 4: Trade-off 판단
- Stage 5: Edge Case 발견
- Stage 6: 방향 수정
- Stage 7: 최종 결정

At each stage: narrate the expert inner monologue → ask sharp questions → wait for user response → validate and move on.

**FastAPI-specific probes** (add these at the relevant stages if stack is FastAPI):

- Stage 0: "이 엔드포인트가 `service/`를 거쳐야 해, 아니면 `crud/`를 직접 불러도 돼?"
- Stage 1: "응답 schema가 몇 tier 필요해? Input / Out만? 아니면 DB domain model도?"
- Stage 4: "route → service → crud 레이어를 지키면 뭘 포기하게 돼? 그게 합리적이야?"
- Stage 5: "DB 세션 커밋/롤백이 어느 레이어 책임이야? `crud`가 commit하면 안 되는 이유는?"

At Stage 7, after the user writes the final decision sentence, delete any existing `/tmp/spec.md` then write a fresh one:

```markdown
# Implementation Spec — [feature name]

## Problem
[from Stage 0: who uses it, failure cost, scope boundary]

## Decision
[Stage 7 final decision sentence]

## Why
[Stage 4 trade-off rationale]

## Edge Cases to Handle
[Stage 5 findings]

## What NOT to build
[Stage 0 out-of-scope items]

## Implementation Constraints
[all constraints from stages 1–6]

### Code Style (FastAPI — mandatory if stack detected in Phase 0)
- Project structure: api/ → service/ → crud/ (routes never call crud directly)
- Schemas: 3 tiers — {Feature}Create (input), {Feature}Out (response), SQLAlchemy model (DB)
- Dependencies: Annotated aliases in api/deps.py — DBSession, CurrentUser
- Pydantic v2: ConfigDict(from_attributes=True), model_dump(), model_validate()
- App factory: lifespan context manager, not @app.on_event
- Errors: domain exceptions in service/, handler registered in main.py
- No HTTPException inside service layer
- response_model= explicit when return type differs from serialized type

## Files to touch
[expected file list following fastapi structure:
 api/v1/endpoints/, service/, crud/, models/, schemas/, core/config.py]
```

Then show the spec as a human-readable design summary — not the raw markdown, but a structured explanation:

```
## 설계 요약

### 뭘 만드나
[한 줄 — 서비스가 하는 일]

### 엔드포인트
[URL과 각각 한 줄 설명]

### 핵심 결정
[Stage 4의 옵션 비교 중 가장 중요한 것 2~3개]
- Option A vs B: [뭘 선택했고 왜, 재고 조건]

### 내가 가정하고 있는 것들
[설계에 묵시적으로 깔린 가정 — 지금 고쳐줘, 아니면 그대로 진행할게]
- [가정 1]
- [가정 2]

### 안 만드는 것
[What NOT to Build 항목 — 왜 제외했는지 한 줄씩]

### 파일 구조
[Files to Create 트리]
```

Then ask:

```
설계 리뷰해줘. 가정 중 틀린 거 있으면 지금 말해줘. 없으면 "응" 하면 구현 시작할게.
```

Wait for confirmation or adjustments before Phase 2. If adjustments: update `/tmp/spec.md` and re-show the summary.

---

## Phase 2 — Iterate (Implementation)

Run `/iterate` for each feature/task identified in Phase 1.

If spec covers a single feature:
```bash
{ cat /tmp/spec.md; echo -e "\nAfter implementation, write a summary of all changes to /tmp/reply.txt"; } | codex exec --full-auto
cat /tmp/reply.txt
```

If spec covers multiple features, run `/iterate <feature>` for each one in priority order, showing the user the result between each and asking to continue.

After all iterations complete, proceed to Phase 3.

---

## Phase 3 — README Polish

Read the current `README.md` and compare against the actual code. Check each of these:

1. **Architecture diagram vs routes** — every URL in the diagram must match actual route decorators:
   ```bash
   grep -rn "@router\." app/api/
   ```

2. **False claims** — any `→ validate X` or `→ check Y` in the diagram that isn't in the code must be removed.

3. **Response shape accuracy** — every `→ returns: {...}` must match the actual `response_model` fields.

4. **Trade-offs table** — every significant design decision from Stage 4 must have a row.

5. **AI Leverage table** — every file touched in Phase 2 must be listed with verification method.

6. **§If More Time** — anything ruled out in Stage 0 must appear here as a named P2 item.

Fix any gaps found, then proceed to Phase 4.

---

## Phase 4 — Final Gate (must all pass before commit)

First, detect the source root: check if `src/` or `app/` exists and use that as `$SRC`.

Run these and show each result:

```bash
SRC=$([ -d src ] && echo src || echo app)
uv run pytest -v
uv run ruff check $SRC/
```

| Check | Command | Must pass |
|-------|---------|-----------|
| Tests green | `uv run pytest -v` | 0 failed |
| Lint clean | `uv run ruff check $SRC/` | no output |
| Layer separation | `grep -rn "from $SRC.store\|from $SRC.crud" $SRC/routes.py 2>/dev/null` | no direct store/crud import in routes |
| URLs match README | `grep -rn "@router\." $SRC/` | matches architecture diagram |
| No false claims | read README §Architecture | every claim exists in code |
| Response shapes | read README §Architecture | matches response_model fields |
| Trade-offs complete | read README §4 | every Stage 4 decision documented |
| AI Leverage complete | read README §3 | every Phase 2 file listed |

Show result as:

```
Phase 4 — Final Gate

✓ pytest: N passed
✓ ruff: clean
✓ Layer separation: routes don't import crud directly
✓ README URLs consistent
✓ No false claims
✓ Response shapes accurate
✓ Trade-offs documented
✓ AI Leverage complete

Ready to commit. 커밋 메시지 확인 후 push.
```

If any check fails: fix it before showing this summary. Do not mark ✓ for a check you haven't run.

---

### Phase 4B — Spec Adherence (Evaluator Agent)

Runs only after **all mechanical checks above pass**.

Spawn a bounded evaluator subagent with this exact prompt:

```
Read /tmp/spec.md and the output of `git diff main` (run it first).

Return a scored checklist. For each item, mark PASS or FAIL and cite file:line if PASS.

Checklist:
1. Each ## Decision item — is it implemented?
2. Each ## Edge Cases to Handle item — is it handled?
3. Layer separation — no `from.*crud` imports in routes.py or api/ files
4. Response schemas omit child lists (PlanStepOut has no patches, PatchProposalOut has no checks)
5. LLM input schemas are not domain models (PlanStepInput / PatchProposalInput used, not PlanStep / PatchProposal)
6. Every GuardrailCheck.reason cites a specific rule ("per AGENTS.md R4", not generic "error")

Output format:
  ✓ [item] — [file:line]
  ✗ [item] — NOT found / describe gap
```

If any ✗ in the evaluator output → fix the gap, re-run mechanical checks, re-run evaluator.
Only proceed to commit when evaluator returns all ✓.

---
name: assignment
description: End-to-end assignment workflow — loads tech stack constraints → /think (design, 7 stages) → /iterate (Codex implements) → README polish → final gate. Usage: /assignment <assignment description or path>
allowed-tools: Read, Write, Edit, Bash, Agent, Task, TodoWrite, TodoRead
---

# Assignment Workflow

Feature/Task: **$ARGUMENTS**

Four phases in sequence. Do not skip or reorder.

---

## Phase 0 — Load Stack Constraints

Before any design begins, detect the tech stack and load the relevant skill as active constraints.

**Detection**: read the assignment spec (if a file path is given) or infer from $ARGUMENTS.

If the project uses **FastAPI / Python backend**:
→ Read `~/.claude/commands/fastapi.md` in full.
→ All patterns in that file are now **mandatory constraints** for Phase 1 design and Phase 2 implementation.
→ Tell the user:

```
Stack detected: FastAPI + Python
Loaded constraints from /fastapi skill:
  ✓ 3-tier schema (Input / Out / Domain)
  ✓ api/ → service/ → crud/ layer separation
  ✓ Annotated dependency aliases in deps.py
  ✓ Pydantic v2 (ConfigDict, model_dump, model_validate)
  ✓ lifespan instead of on_event
  ✓ Domain exceptions, not HTTPException in service layer

이 제약들이 Phase 1 설계와 Phase 2 구현에 적용돼.
```

If other stacks are detected (Next.js, Go, etc.) — note them and proceed without FastAPI constraints.

---

## Phase 1 — Think (Design)

Run the full `/think` flow for: $ARGUMENTS

Walk through all 8 stages (Stage 0 → Stage 7):
- Stage 0: 첫 궁금증 (5 first curiosity questions)
- Stage 1: 문제 이해
- Stage 2: AI 탐색 설계
- Stage 3: 결과 검증
- Stage 4: Trade-off 판단
- Stage 5: Edge Case 발견
- Stage 6: 방향 수정
- Stage 7: 최종 결정

At each stage: narrate the expert inner monologue → ask sharp questions → wait for user response → validate and move on.

**FastAPI-specific probes** (add these at the relevant stages if stack is FastAPI):

- Stage 0: "이 엔드포인트가 `service/`를 거쳐야 해, 아니면 `crud/`를 직접 불러도 돼?"
- Stage 1: "응답 schema가 몇 tier 필요해? Input / Out만? 아니면 DB domain model도?"
- Stage 4: "route → service → crud 레이어를 지키면 뭘 포기하게 돼? 그게 합리적이야?"
- Stage 5: "DB 세션 커밋/롤백이 어느 레이어 책임이야? `crud`가 commit하면 안 되는 이유는?"

At Stage 7, after the user writes the final decision sentence, delete any existing `/tmp/spec.md` then write a fresh one:

```markdown
# Implementation Spec — [feature name]

## Problem
[from Stage 0: who uses it, failure cost, scope boundary]

## Decision
[Stage 7 final decision sentence]

## Why
[Stage 4 trade-off rationale]

## Edge Cases to Handle
[Stage 5 findings]

## What NOT to build
[Stage 0 out-of-scope items]

## Implementation Constraints
[all constraints from stages 1–6]

### Code Style (FastAPI — mandatory if stack detected in Phase 0)
- Project structure: api/ → service/ → crud/ (routes never call crud directly)
- Schemas: 3 tiers — {Feature}Create (input), {Feature}Out (response), SQLAlchemy model (DB)
- Dependencies: Annotated aliases in api/deps.py — DBSession, CurrentUser
- Pydantic v2: ConfigDict(from_attributes=True), model_dump(), model_validate()
- App factory: lifespan context manager, not @app.on_event
- Errors: domain exceptions in service/, handler registered in main.py
- No HTTPException inside service layer
- response_model= explicit when return type differs from serialized type

## Files to touch
[expected file list following fastapi structure:
 api/v1/endpoints/, service/, crud/, models/, schemas/, core/config.py]
```

Then show the spec as a human-readable design summary — not the raw markdown, but a structured explanation:

```
## 설계 요약

### 뭘 만드나
[한 줄 — 서비스가 하는 일]

### 엔드포인트
[URL과 각각 한 줄 설명]

### 핵심 결정
[Stage 4의 옵션 비교 중 가장 중요한 것 2~3개]
- Option A vs B: [뭘 선택했고 왜, 재고 조건]

### 내가 가정하고 있는 것들
[설계에 묵시적으로 깔린 가정 — 지금 고쳐줘, 아니면 그대로 진행할게]
- [가정 1]
- [가정 2]

### 안 만드는 것
[What NOT to Build 항목 — 왜 제외했는지 한 줄씩]

### 파일 구조
[Files to Create 트리]
```

Then ask:

```
설계 리뷰해줘. 가정 중 틀린 거 있으면 지금 말해줘. 없으면 "응" 하면 구현 시작할게.
```

Wait for confirmation or adjustments before Phase 2. If adjustments: update `/tmp/spec.md` and re-show the summary.

Once confirmed, run the spec validator before handing off to Codex:

Spawn a subagent with `~/.claude/agents/spec-validator.md` as prompt.
- If STATUS is NEEDS UPDATE: apply the exact additions the validator provides to `/tmp/spec.md`, show the user what was added, then proceed.
- If STATUS is READY: proceed immediately.

---

## Phase 2 — Iterate (Implementation)

Run `/iterate` for each feature/task identified in Phase 1.

If spec covers a single feature:
```bash
{ cat /tmp/spec.md; echo -e "\nAfter implementation, write a summary of all changes to /tmp/reply.txt"; } | codex exec --full-auto
cat /tmp/reply.txt
```

If spec covers multiple features, run `/iterate <feature>` for each one in priority order, showing the user the result between each and asking to continue.

After all iterations complete, proceed to Phase 3.

---

## Phase 3 — README Polish

Read the current `README.md` and compare against the actual code. Check each of these:

1. **Architecture diagram vs routes** — every URL in the diagram must match actual route decorators:
   ```bash
   grep -rn "@router\." app/api/
   ```

2. **False claims** — any `→ validate X` or `→ check Y` in the diagram that isn't in the code must be removed.

3. **Response shape accuracy** — every `→ returns: {...}` must match the actual `response_model` fields.

4. **Trade-offs table** — every significant design decision from Stage 4 must have a row.

5. **AI Leverage table** — every file touched in Phase 2 must be listed with verification method.

6. **§If More Time** — anything ruled out in Stage 0 must appear here as a named P2 item.

Fix any gaps found, then proceed to Phase 4.

---

## Phase 4 — Final Gate (must all pass before commit)

First, detect the source root: check if `src/` or `app/` exists and use that as `$SRC`.

Run these and show each result:

```bash
SRC=$([ -d src ] && echo src || echo app)
uv run pytest -v
uv run ruff check $SRC/
```

| Check | Command | Must pass |
|-------|---------|-----------|
| Tests green | `uv run pytest -v` | 0 failed |
| Lint clean | `uv run ruff check $SRC/` | no output |
| Layer separation | `grep -rn "from $SRC.store\|from $SRC.crud" $SRC/routes.py 2>/dev/null` | no direct store/crud import in routes |
| URLs match README | `grep -rn "@router\." $SRC/` | matches architecture diagram |
| No false claims | read README §Architecture | every claim exists in code |
| Response shapes | read README §Architecture | matches response_model fields |
| Trade-offs complete | read README §4 | every Stage 4 decision documented |
| AI Leverage complete | read README §3 | every Phase 2 file listed |

Show result as:

```
Phase 4 — Final Gate

✓ pytest: N passed
✓ ruff: clean
✓ Layer separation: routes don't import crud directly
✓ README URLs consistent
✓ No false claims
✓ Response shapes accurate
✓ Trade-offs documented
✓ AI Leverage complete

Ready to commit. 커밋 메시지 확인 후 push.
```

If any check fails: fix it before showing this summary. Do not mark ✓ for a check you haven't run.

---

### Phase 4B — Spec Adherence (Evaluator Agent)

Runs only after **all mechanical checks above pass**.

Spawn a bounded evaluator subagent with this exact prompt:

```
Read /tmp/spec.md and the output of `git diff main` (run it first).

Return a scored checklist. For each item, mark PASS or FAIL and cite file:line if PASS.

Checklist:
1. Each ## Decision item — is it implemented?
2. Each ## Edge Cases to Handle item — is it handled?
3. Layer separation — no `from.*crud` imports in routes.py or api/ files
4. Response schemas omit child lists (PlanStepOut has no patches, PatchProposalOut has no checks)
5. LLM input schemas are not domain models (PlanStepInput / PatchProposalInput used, not PlanStep / PatchProposal)
6. Every GuardrailCheck.reason cites a specific rule ("per AGENTS.md R4", not generic "error")

Output format:
  ✓ [item] — [file:line]
  ✗ [item] — NOT found / describe gap
```

If any ✗ in the evaluator output → fix the gap, re-run mechanical checks, re-run evaluator.
Only proceed to commit when evaluator returns all ✓.

content.text:
맞아, 핵심 문제야. `codex exec --full-auto`는 완전 자율이라 구현 중에 질문이 불가능해. Codex가 모호한 부분을 만나면 그냥 자기 판단으로 채워.

선택지가 두 가지야:

**Option A — 내가 직접 구현 (worktree 격리)**
- Claude (나)가 파일 하나씩 구현
- 불확실한 부분 만나면 바로 물어봄
- worktree로 격리해서 안전하게 실험 가능
- 단점: 느림

**Option B — Codex를 stage별로 쪼개기**
- models → service → routes → tests 순서로 각 단계마다 Codex 실행
- 단계 사이에 스팟체크 + 질문 가능
- 단점: spec이 완벽해야 각 단계가 정확함. 단계 간 연결이 틀리면 복잡해짐

---

내 생각엔 **Option A가 맞아** — 이 워크플로우의 핵심이 "구현 중 인간 판단 개입"이라면, Codex 자율 실행이랑 근본적으로 충돌해. spec이 아무리 완벽해도 예외 케이스가 나와.

`/iterate`의 Builder 스텝을 Codex가 아니라 Claude가 worktree에서 직접 구현하는 방식으로 바꿀까?

input.old_string:
## Step 1 — Builder (Codex in isolated worktree)

**First: validate the spec.** Spawn a subagent with `~/.claude/agents/spec-validator.md` as prompt.
- If STATUS is NEEDS UPDATE: apply the additions to `/tmp/spec.md`, re-validate, then continue.
- If STATUS is READY: proceed.

**Then: create an isolated git worktree and run Codex from there.**

Detect `PROJECT_SUBDIR` from `## Files to Create` in spec (top-level directory name, e.g. `case-01-session-second`).

```bash
BRANCH="codex/build-$(date +%s)"
WORKTREE="/tmp/codex-wt-$(date +%s)"
git worktree add "$WORKTREE" -b "$BRANCH"
echo "$WORKTREE" > /tmp/codex-worktree.txt
echo "$BRANCH" > /tmp/codex-branch.txt

toolUseResult.originalFile:
---
name: iterate
description: Multi-agent iteration loop with human checkpoints — Pre-build → Builder → Spot-check → Tester → Reviewer → Human. Usage: /iterate <feature description>
---

# Iterate — Build Loop with Human Checkpoints

Feature: $ARGUMENTS

Maximum 3 iterations. Report blockers if still failing after 3.

---

## Checkpoint map

```
[0] Pre-build     ← YOU review scope + add constraints
      ↓
[1] Builder       ← AI implements (worktree isolated)
      ↓
[2] Spot-check    ← YOU review diff for domain issues
      ↓
[3] Tester        ← AI runs pytest + ruff
      ↓
[4] Reviewer      ← AI checks spec alignment
      ↓
[5] Commit        ← YOU approve message + merge
```

Checkpoints [0] and [2] are yours. The rest are agent turns.

---

## Step 0 — Pre-build checkpoint (YOU)

**If `/tmp/spec.md` already exists** (called from `/assignment`): skip straight to Step 1 — spec is already finalized.

Otherwise, read `PLAN.md` entry for this feature (if exists), then show:

```
## Pre-build: [feature name]

### Scope
- Files: [list]
- Behavior: [one sentence]

### Your turn — before I spawn Builder:
1. Anything to add to the scope? (domain constraints, edge cases I missed)
2. Any "watch out for X" from your experience?
3. Ready to build? (yes / adjust scope first)
```

Wait for explicit "yes" or adjustments. Write confirmed scope to `/tmp/spec.md`.

---

## Step 1 — Builder (Codex in isolated worktree)

**First: validate the spec.** Spawn a subagent with `~/.claude/agents/spec-validator.md` as prompt.
- If STATUS is NEEDS UPDATE: apply the additions to `/tmp/spec.md`, re-validate, then continue.
- If STATUS is READY: proceed.

**Then: create an isolated git worktree and run Codex from there.**

Detect `PROJECT_SUBDIR` from `## Files to Create` in spec (top-level directory name, e.g. `case-01-session-second`).

```bash
BRANCH="codex/build-$(date +%s)"
WORKTREE="/tmp/codex-wt-$(date +%s)"
git worktree add "$WORKTREE" -b "$BRANCH"
echo "$WORKTREE" > /tmp/codex-worktree.txt
echo "$BRANCH" > /tmp/codex-branch.txt

cd "$WORKTREE/$PROJECT_SUBDIR"
{ cat /tmp/spec.md; cat ~/.claude/agents/builder.md; echo -e "\nAfter implementation, write a summary of all changes to /tmp/reply.txt"; } | codex exec --full-auto
cat /tmp/reply.txt
```

**DO NOT write implementation files yourself.** Codex does all file writing.

Use the output of `/tmp/reply.txt` as the Builder result for Step 2.

---

## Step 2 — Spot-check checkpoint (YOU)

After Builder completes, show:

```
## Spot-check: Builder finished

### Files changed
[list with one-line summary of what changed in each]

### Diff highlights (things worth your eye)
- [any non-obvious decision Builder made]
- [anything that touches the hard problem]
- [any assumption Builder encoded in code]

### Your turn:
1. Any domain issue in the diff? (e.g. missing edge case, wrong field name)
2. Any test case you want added before I run the suite?
3. Good to proceed to Tester? (yes / request change)
```

If change requested: send specific fix back to Builder (no iteration count increment — this is a refinement, not a failure).
If yes: proceed to Step 3.

---

## Step 3 — Tester (Codex)

Run tests via Codex in the same worktree. **DO NOT run tests yourself.**

```bash
WORKTREE=$(cat /tmp/codex-worktree.txt)
PROJECT_SUBDIR="<same as Step 1>"
cd "$WORKTREE/$PROJECT_SUBDIR"
uv sync --quiet  # ensure deps installed without network output
{ cat ~/.claude/agents/tester.md; echo -e "\nWorking directory: $(pwd)\nWrite the Tester Report to /tmp/tester-report.txt"; } | codex exec --full-auto
cat /tmp/tester-report.txt
```

If VERDICT FAIL: pass exact error output to Builder → iteration +1 → back to Step 1.

---

## Step 4 — Reviewer Agent

Read `~/.claude/agents/reviewer.md` and spawn a subagent with that as the prompt, plus list of changed files.

Only run after Tester PASS.

If VERDICT FAIL: pass gap list to Builder → iteration +1 → back to Step 1.

---

## Step 5 — Commit checkpoint (YOU)

Both agents PASS. Show:

```
## Ready to commit

Iterations: N/3
Tests: [N]/[total] passing
Review: all gaps resolved

### What was built
[2–3 sentences]

### Files changed
- [file]: [what changed]

### Assumptions encoded in this change
[anything non-obvious that future devs should know]

### Commit message
feat: [description]

Approve? (yes / edit message / skip)
```

If approved, merge the worktree branch and clean up:

```bash
WORKTREE=$(cat /tmp/codex-worktree.txt)
BRANCH=$(cat /tmp/codex-branch.txt)
git merge "$BRANCH"
git worktree remove "$WORKTREE"
git branch -d "$BRANCH"
rm -f /tmp/codex-worktree.txt /tmp/codex-branch.txt
```

---

## Iteration limit

If iteration 3 ends with FAIL:

```
## Blocked after 3 iterations

Last failure type: Tester | Reviewer
Error: [exact output]

Your options:
1. Narrow scope — tell me what to cut
2. Clarify constraint — something in the spec is ambiguous
3. Override — commit as-is with known gap documented
4. Skip — move to next feature in PLAN.md
```

완료. 흐름이 이렇게 바뀌어:

```
Step 1:
  1. spec-validator 실행 (gap 있으면 보강)
  2. git worktree 생성 + SPEC.md / BUILDER_RULES.md 복사
  3. "이 디렉토리에서 codex 실행해" → 유저가 Codex 터미널로 넘어감
  4. 유저가 Codex랑 대화하며 구현 (질문/답변 가능)
  5. "완료" 하면 Claude로 돌아와서 diff 확인

Step 2: Spot-check (유저 리뷰)
Step 3: Tester (Codex exec으로 pytest)
Step 4: Reviewer (Claude agent)
Step 5: merge + worktree 정리
```

지금 현재 과제 (`case-01-session-second`) — 이미 구현이 되어 있는데, README 작업하고 커밋할까?

content.text:
응, 전체 흐름 유저 입장에서 설명할게:

---

```
1. /assignment <과제 설명 또는 파일 경로>
   → Phase 0: 스택 감지 (FastAPI 등)
   → Phase 1: /think 8단계 설계 (나랑 대화)
   → 설계 요약 보여줌 + "틀린 가정 있으면 말해줘"
   → spec-validator 자동 실행 (필드명 누락 등 잡아냄)
   → "응" 하면 Phase 2로

2. Phase 2에서 내가 이렇게 말해줘:
   "worktree 준비됐어: /tmp/codex-wt-xxx/case-01-...
    cd 거기로 가서 codex 실행해줘"

3. 유저가 새 터미널에서:
   cd /tmp/codex-wt-xxx/case-01-...
   codex
   → SPEC.md, BUILDER_RULES.md 참조시켜서 구현
   → 모호한 거 있으면 Codex랑 대화하면서 결정
   → 다 되면 Claude 탭으로 돌아와서 "완료"

4. 내가 diff 보고 Spot-check 보여줌
   → 수정 원하면 다시 Codex 탭으로
   → 괜찮으면 "응"

5. Tester 자동 실행 (codex로 pytest/ruff)
6. Reviewer 자동 실행 (Claude agent)
7. 커밋 메시지 보여주고 승인 받음
8. worktree merge + 정리
```

---

핵심은 **Claude 탭 ↔ Codex 탭** 을 오가는 거야:
- **Claude 탭**: 설계, 스팟체크, 테스트 결과 확인, 커밋 승인
- **Codex 탭**: 실제 구현 (질문하면서)

지금 `case-01-session-second`는 이미 구현됐으니까 README → 커밋만 남았어. 할까?

---
name: diff-review
description: Explain implementation diffs, summarize changed behavior, and prioritize what reviewers should inspect first. Use when the user asks for /diff-review, code review, diff explanation, review priorities, or what to check before merge.
metadata:
  short-description: Prioritized diff review
---

# Diff Review

Run a local implementation diff review. Do not modify files or post comments unless the user explicitly asks.

## Diff Collection

Start with:

```bash
git status --short
git diff --stat HEAD
git diff --find-renames HEAD
git ls-files --others --exclude-standard
```

If the user gives a base ref, branch, or commit, use `git diff --stat <ref>...HEAD` and `git diff --find-renames <ref>...HEAD`.

If the user gives a PR number and GitHub CLI is available, use `gh pr diff <number>`.

## Review Method

1. Identify implementation intent from the diff.
2. Explain what changed by behavior or layer.

input.content:
---
name: implement
description: Pick up a Claude /iterate session and implement the spec interactively. Use when Claude has set up a worktree and you want to start building.
metadata:
  short-description: Implement from /iterate spec
---

# Implement — Pick Up from Claude /iterate

You are implementing a spec that Claude prepared. Your job is to build exactly what the spec says, ask when uncertain, and nothing more.

## Setup

Read these files in the current directory (Claude placed them here):

```bash
cat SPEC.md          # what to build — follow exactly
cat BUILDER_RULES.md # constraints — follow exactly
```

If either file is missing, tell the user and stop.

## Implementation order

Build in this order to catch design issues early:

1. **models.py** — all schema classes first. Show the user and ask: "필드명 맞아?"
2. **store.py** — in-memory state only
3. **guardrails.py** — deterministic rules, no LLM
4. **llm.py** — Anthropic integration + mock mode
5. **service.py** — business logic, domain exceptions
6. **routes.py** — API layer only, no store imports
7. **main.py** — load_dotenv first, include router
8. **efood/AGENTS.md** (or brand dir) — brand rules doc
9. **tests/** — guardrail unit tests + E2E flow

## When to ask

Stop and ask the user when:
- A field name or type isn't specified in SPEC.md
- An endpoint's response shape is ambiguous
- Two approaches are equally valid and spec doesn't say which
- You're about to make an assumption that changes the public API

**Do not ask** about:
- Internal variable names
- Helper function structure
- Import ordering
- Things BUILDER_RULES.md already answers

## Rules from BUILDER_RULES.md (summary)

- routes.py → service.py → store.py (no skipping layers)
- Response Out schemas omit child collections
- LLM calls: tool_use + tool_choice, max_tokens≥4096, response.content[0].input
- Mock mode when ANTHROPIC_API_KEY is empty
- load_dotenv() before local imports in main.py
- logging not print

## When done

Run:
```bash
python3 -m compileall -q src/ tests/ 2>&1
```

Then tell the user:
```
구현 완료. Claude 탭으로 돌아가서 "완료"라고 말해줘.
변경된 파일: [list]
```

---
name: iterate
description: Multi-agent iteration loop with human checkpoints — Pre-build → Builder → Spot-check → Tester → Reviewer → Human. Usage: /iterate <feature description>
---

# Iterate — Build Loop with Human Checkpoints

Feature: $ARGUMENTS

Maximum 3 iterations. Report blockers if still failing after 3.

---

## Checkpoint map

```
[0] Pre-build     ← YOU review scope + add constraints
      ↓
[1] Builder       ← AI implements (worktree isolated)
      ↓
[2] Spot-check    ← YOU review diff for domain issues
      ↓
[3] Tester        ← AI runs pytest + ruff
      ↓
[4] Reviewer      ← AI checks spec alignment
      ↓
[5] Commit        ← YOU approve message + merge
```

Checkpoints [0] and [2] are yours. The rest are agent turns.

---

## Step 0 — Pre-build checkpoint (YOU)

**If `/tmp/spec.md` already exists** (called from `/assignment`): skip straight to Step 1 — spec is already finalized.

Otherwise, read `PLAN.md` entry for this feature (if exists), then show:

```
## Pre-build: [feature name]

### Scope
- Files: [list]
- Behavior: [one sentence]

### Your turn — before I spawn Builder:
1. Anything to add to the scope? (domain constraints, edge cases I missed)
2. Any "watch out for X" from your experience?
3. Ready to build? (yes / adjust scope first)
```

Wait for explicit "yes" or adjustments. Write confirmed scope to `/tmp/spec.md`.

---

## Step 1 — Builder (Codex interactive in isolated worktree)

**First: validate the spec.** Spawn a subagent with `~/.claude/agents/spec-validator.md` as prompt.
- If STATUS is NEEDS UPDATE: apply the additions to `/tmp/spec.md`, re-validate, then continue.
- If STATUS is READY: proceed.

**Then: create an isolated git worktree and hand off to the user.**

Detect `PROJECT_SUBDIR` from `## Files to Create` in spec (top-level directory name).

```bash
BRANCH="codex/build-$(date +%s)"
WORKTREE="/tmp/codex-wt-$(date +%s)"
git worktree add "$WORKTREE" -b "$BRANCH"
echo "$WORKTREE" > /tmp/codex-worktree.txt
echo "$BRANCH" > /tmp/codex-branch.txt

# spec과 builder 규칙을 worktree에 복사해서 Codex가 참조 가능하게
cp /tmp/spec.md "$WORKTREE/$PROJECT_SUBDIR/SPEC.md"
cp ~/.claude/agents/builder.md "$WORKTREE/$PROJECT_SUBDIR/BUILDER_RULES.md"
```

Then tell the user:

```
worktree 준비됐어: $WORKTREE/$PROJECT_SUBDIR
브랜치: $BRANCH

Codex 터미널에서 이 디렉토리로 이동해서 구현해줘:
  cd $WORKTREE/$PROJECT_SUBDIR
  codex

SPEC.md와 BUILDER_RULES.md가 같은 디렉토리에 있어 — Codex에게 참조시켜.
구현 끝나면 여기로 돌아와서 "완료"라고 말해줘.
```

Wait for the user to return after implementing in Codex. Then read the diff:

```bash
git -C "$WORKTREE" diff HEAD
```

Use the diff as the Builder result for Step 2.

---

## Step 2 — Spot-check checkpoint (YOU)

After Builder completes, show:

```
## Spot-check: Builder finished

### Files changed
[list with one-line summary of what changed in each]

### Diff highlights (things worth your eye)
- [any non-obvious decision Builder made]
- [anything that touches the hard problem]
- [any assumption Builder encoded in code]

### Your turn:
1. Any domain issue in the diff? (e.g. missing edge case, wrong field name)
2. Any test case you want added before I run the suite?
3. Good to proceed to Tester? (yes / request change)
```

If change requested: send specific fix back to Builder (no iteration count increment — this is a refinement, not a failure).
If yes: proceed to Step 3.

---

## Step 3 — Tester (Codex)

Run tests via Codex in the same worktree. **DO NOT run tests yourself.**

```bash
WORKTREE=$(cat /tmp/codex-worktree.txt)
PROJECT_SUBDIR="<same as Step 1>"
cd "$WORKTREE/$PROJECT_SUBDIR"
uv sync --quiet  # ensure deps installed without network output
{ cat ~/.claude/agents/tester.md; echo -e "\nWorking directory: $(pwd)\nWrite the Tester Report to /tmp/tester-report.txt"; } | codex exec --full-auto
cat /tmp/tester-report.txt
```

If VERDICT FAIL: pass exact error output to Builder → iteration +1 → back to Step 1.

---

## Step 4 — Reviewer Agent

Read `~/.claude/agents/reviewer.md` and spawn a subagent with that as the prompt, plus list of changed files.

Only run after Tester PASS.

If VERDICT FAIL: pass gap list to Builder → iteration +1 → back to Step 1.

---

## Step 5 — Commit checkpoint (YOU)

Both agents PASS. Show:

```
## Ready to commit

Iterations: N/3
Tests: [N]/[total] passing
Review: all gaps resolved

### What was built
[2–3 sentences]

### Files changed
- [file]: [what changed]

### Assumptions encoded in this change
[anything non-obvious that future devs should know]

### Commit message
feat: [description]

Approve? (yes / edit message / skip)
```

If approved, merge the worktree branch and clean up:

```bash
WORKTREE=$(cat /tmp/codex-worktree.txt)
BRANCH=$(cat /tmp/codex-branch.txt)
git merge "$BRANCH"
git worktree remove "$WORKTREE"
git branch -d "$BRANCH"
rm -f /tmp/codex-worktree.txt /tmp/codex-branch.txt
```

---

## Iteration limit

If iteration 3 ends with FAIL:

```
## Blocked after 3 iterations

Last failure type: Tester | Reviewer
Error: [exact output]

Your options:
1. Narrow scope — tell me what to cut
2. Clarify constraint — something in the spec is ambiguous
3. Override — commit as-is with known gap documented
4. Skip — move to next feature in PLAN.md
```

```

builder.md   ──copy──▶ $WORKTREE/case-01-.../BUILDER_RULES.md

cat SPEC.md BUILDER_RULES.md | codex

---
name: implement
description: Pick up a Claude /iterate session and implement the spec interactively. Use when Claude has set up a worktree and you want to start building.
metadata:
  short-description: Implement from /iterate spec
---

# Implement — Pick Up from Claude /iterate

You are implementing a spec that Claude prepared. Your job is to build exactly what the spec says, ask when uncertain, and nothing more.

## Setup

Read these files in the current directory (Claude placed them here):

```bash
cat SPEC.md          # what to build — follow exactly
cat BUILDER_RULES.md # constraints — follow exactly
```

If either file is missing, tell the user and stop.

## Implementation order

Build in this order to catch design issues early:

1. **models.py** — all schema classes first. Show the user and ask: "필드명 맞아?"
2. **store.py** — in-memory state only
3. **guardrails.py** — deterministic rules, no LLM
4. **llm.py** — Anthropic integration + mock mode
5. **service.py** — business logic, domain exceptions
6. **routes.py** — API layer only, no store imports
7. **main.py** — load_dotenv first, include router
8. **efood/AGENTS.md** (or brand dir) — brand rules doc
9. **tests/** — guardrail unit tests + E2E flow

## When to ask

Stop and ask the user when:
- A field name or type isn't specified in SPEC.md
- An endpoint's response shape is ambiguous
- Two approaches are equally valid and spec doesn't say which
- You're about to make an assumption that changes the public API

**Do not ask** about:
- Internal variable names
- Helper function structure
- Import ordering
- Things BUILDER_RULES.md already answers

## Rules from BUILDER_RULES.md (summary)

- routes.py → service.py → store.py (no skipping layers)
- Response Out schemas omit child collections
- LLM calls: tool_use + tool_choice, max_tokens≥4096, response.content[0].input
- Mock mode when ANTHROPIC_API_KEY is empty
- load_dotenv() before local imports in main.py
- logging not print

## When done

Run:
```bash
python3 -m compileall -q src/ tests/ 2>&1
```

Then tell the user:
```
구현 완료. Claude 탭으로 돌아가서 "완료"라고 말해줘.
변경된 파일: [list]
```

# Implementation Spec — case-01-session-second

## Problem
개발자가 AI에게 코드 변경을 요청 → LLM이 계획/diff를 제안 → 브랜드 정책(AGENTS.md R1~R5)에 따라 guardrail이 merge 가능 여부 판단. 실패 비용: 위반 코드가 production 배포.

## Decision
efood 브랜드의 AI 코딩 세션을 5개 endpoint로 호스팅하는 FastAPI 서비스 구현. LLM(Claude)은 plan/patch 생성에만 사용, R1~R5 guardrail은 전부 deterministic regex. 스토리지 in-memory dict, check URL은 `/patches/{patchId}/check`로 단순화.

## Why
guardrail이 비결정적이면 동일 patch에 다른 결과가 나올 수 있음. R1~R5는 regex로 100% 커버 가능 — LLM 쓸 이유 없음. `/patches/{patchId}/check`에서 session_id는 UUID patch_id가 globally unique라 불필요한 중복 scope.

## Edge Cases to Handle
- step_id가 session에 없으면 404
- patch_id가 없으면 404
- /check 중복 호출 → checks 덮어쓰기 (idempotent)
- brand 범위 외 값 → Pydantic 입력단에서 422
- API key 없을 때 → mock 응답 (테스트 가능)
- 빈 patch → R1~R5 전부 pass

## What NOT to Build
- 인증/권한
- 영속성 (DB, Redis)
- 멀티브랜드 라우팅 (brand 필드는 있지만 efood만 실제 구현)
- 비동기 큐 / streaming LLM
- OTEL / distributed tracing (trace_id 필드만 포함)

## Implementation Constraints

### URL 구조
```
POST /sessions
POST /sessions/{id}/plan
POST /sessions/{id}/patches          (body: {step_id})
POST /patches/{patchId}/check
GET  /sessions/{id}
GET  /health
```

### Schema (3-tier) — 필드명 정확히 지켜야 함

```python
# Input (caller-provided only)
class SessionCreate(BaseModel):
    title: str
    description: str
    brand: Brand  # Literal["efood", "glovo", "talabat"]

class PatchCreate(BaseModel):
    step_id: UUID

# LLM-facing (never pass domain models to LLM)
class PlanStepInput(BaseModel):
...
</persisted-output>

# Implementation Spec — case-01-session-second

## Problem
개발자가 AI에게 코드 변경을 요청 → LLM이 계획/diff를 제안 → 브랜드 정책(AGENTS.md R1~R5)에 따라 guardrail이 merge 가능 여부 판단. 실패 비용: 위반 코드가 production 배포.

## Decision
efood 브랜드의 AI 코딩 세션을 5개 endpoint로 호스팅하는 FastAPI 서비스 구현. LLM(Claude)은 plan/patch 생성에만 사용, R1~R5 guardrail은 전부 deterministic regex. 스토리지 in-memory dict, check URL은 `/patches/{patchId}/check`로 단순화.

## Why
guardrail이 비결정적이면 동일 patch에 다른 결과가 나올 수 있음. R1~R5는 regex로 100% 커버 가능 — LLM 쓸 이유 없음. `/patches/{patchId}/check`에서 session_id는 UUID patch_id가 globally unique라 불필요한 중복 scope.

## Edge Cases to Handle
- step_id가 session에 없으면 404
- patch_id가 없으면 404
- /check 중복 호출 → checks 덮어쓰기 (idempotent)
- brand 범위 외 값 → Pydantic 입력단에서 422
- API key 없을 때 → mock 응답 (테스트 가능)
- 빈 patch → R1~R5 전부 pass

## What NOT to Build
- 인증/권한
- 영속성 (DB, Redis)
- 멀티브랜드 라우팅 (brand 필드는 있지만 efood만 실제 구현)
- 비동기 큐 / streaming LLM
- OTEL / distributed tracing (trace_id 필드만 포함)

## Implementation Constraints

### URL 구조
```
POST /sessions
POST /sessions/{id}/plan
POST /sessions/{id}/patches          (body: {step_id})
POST /patches/{patchId}/check
GET  /sessions/{id}
GET  /health
```

### Schema (3-tier) — 필드명 정확히 지켜야 함

```python
# Input (caller-provided only)
class SessionCreate(BaseModel):
    title: str
    description: str
    brand: Brand  # Literal["efood", "glovo", "talabat"]

class PatchCreate(BaseModel):
    step_id: UUID

# LLM-facing (never pass domain models to LLM)
class PlanStepInput(BaseModel):
    description: str
    target_files: list[str]

class PatchProposalInput(BaseModel):
    diff: str

# Response — only what this endpoint created, NO child collections
class PlanStepOut(BaseModel):        # POST /plan 응답 — patches 필드 없음
    id: UUID
    description: str
    target_files: list[str]

class PatchProposalOut(BaseModel):   # POST /patches 응답 — checks 필드 없음
    id: UUID
    step_id: UUID
    diff: str
    created_at: datetime

class GuardrailCheck(BaseModel):     # POST /patches/{id}/check 응답 — 5개 룰 전부
    ruleId: str                      # "R1"~"R5"
    severity: Literal["BLOCK", "WARN", "INFO"]
    result: Literal["pass", "fail"]  # pass도 반드시 포함 — violation만 리턴 금지
    reason: str                      # "per AGENTS.md R4" 형식

# Domain (in-memory store, GET /sessions/{id} 응답)
class PatchProposal(BaseModel):
    id: UUID
    step_id: UUID
    brand: Brand
    diff: str
    checks: list[GuardrailCheck] = []
    created_at: datetime

class PlanStep(BaseModel):
    id: UUID
    description: str
    target_files: list[str]
    patches: list[PatchProposal] = []

class Session(BaseModel):
    id: UUID
    title: str
    description: str
    brand: Brand
    trace_id: UUID                   # 서버가 생성 — 입력 스키마에 없음
    steps: list[PlanStep] = []
    created_at: datetime
```

### Endpoint 응답 shape — 정확히 지켜야 함
- `POST /sessions` → `Session` (steps=[] 빈 상태)
- `POST /sessions/{id}/plan` → `list[PlanStepOut]` (patches 필드 없음)
- `POST /sessions/{id}/patches` → `PatchProposalOut` (checks 필드 없음)
- `POST /patches/{patchId}/check` → `list[GuardrailCheck]` (R1~R5 5개 전부, pass 포함)
- `GET /sessions/{id}` → `Session` (steps→patches→checks 전체 nested)

### LLM 규칙
- tool_use + tool_choice={"type":"tool","name":"output"}
- max_tokens >= 4096
- response.content[0].input 으로 파싱
- API key 없으면 mock 리턴

### Guardrail 규칙
- added line만 검사 (startswith("+"), not startswith("+++"))
- R1 WARN: relative import (from .)
- R2 BLOCK: os.system / subprocess
- R3 WARN: public function without docstring
- R4 BLOCK: print()
- R5 BLOCK: requests.get/post/etc

### Code Style (FastAPI)
- layer: routes.py → service 함수 → store.py
- Pydantic v2: ConfigDict(from_attributes=True), model_dump(), model_validate()
- load_dotenv() in main.py before local imports
- logging not print
- no HTTPException in service layer

## Files to Create

case-01-session-second/

# 2. [second step]

# 3. [key step — the one that demonstrates the core behavior]

# 4. full state

```
